Incomplete Dyson swarm surrounds a bloated red giant, billions of hexagonal mirrors catching crimson light. Construction drones drift between panels like microscopic gnats against the star's burning surface.
Ancient Dyson sphere fragments orbit a dying white dwarf, crystalline alien panels cracked and dark. A salvage fleet harvests sections the size of continents, their ships barely visible specks.
Ringworld encircles a yellow sun, interior surface studded with pinprick cities and visible storm systems. The structure's shadow falls across three moons caught in its gravitational embrace.

Battle-scarred orbital ring wraps a gas giant, corroded metal sections torn away revealing interior superstructure. Lightning from the planet below arcs upward, grounding against the ring's damaged hull.
Orbital elevator pierces cloud layers, its carbon-composite cable vanishing into orbital haze above. Climber pods inch upward like beads on a thread, dwarfed by the equatorial city sprawling below.
Ancient space tether hangs broken from orbit, its lower half collapsed across a continent. The surviving upper section still anchors a ruined station, visible as a dark speck against stars.

Rotating space station spans fifty kilometers, residential rings generating artificial gravity through spin. Thousands of docking lights glitter across its surface as freighters queue like patient insects.
Derelict station drifts through an asteroid field, ice-armored hull encrusted with frozen atmosphere. Its broken wheel sections harbor entire ecosystems in the abandoned habitation decks within.
Generation ship crosses the void between stars, a hollow asteroid converted to ark. Through transparent sections, forests and rivers glimmer, clouds forming weather patterns against the interior sky.

Bio-metal generation ship pulses with bioluminescence, living hull regenerating from micrometeorite impacts. Its Garden cylinder rotates visibly, atmospheric moisture creating aurora-like shimmer at the endpoints.

Orbital shipyard constructs a dreadnought in vacuum, the vessel's skeleton exposed across kilometers of framework. Assembly drones swarm the superstructure like luminous plankton, welding sparks creating constellations.
Automated shipyard orbits a resource-rich moon, three vessels in simultaneous construction across ceramic-plated berths. Ore processors feed raw materials through arteries thicker than city streets.

Defense platform guards a wormhole aperture, weapons arrays targeting empty space with patient menace. Battle damage scars the fortress's polished alloy skin, hull plates welded over previous breaches.

Ancient defense satellite network forms a constellation around a dead world, crystalline weapons still tracking phantom threats. One station tumbles slowly, its orbit decaying through millennia of neglect.
Planet-cracking mining rig straddles an ice moon, plasma beams carving through the crust to mineral-rich core. Ore haulers emerge from the wound like blood cells, laden with extracted resources.

Automated mining swarm dismantles a dwarf planet, individual units coordinating like metallic locusts. The industrial cloud has already consumed half the planetoid, leaving geometric excavation patterns behind.

Communications array spreads across a barren moon's surface, dish arrays the size of craters pointed at galactic center. Maintenance bots crawl the receivers, ant-small against the curved reflector surfaces.

Deep-space relay drifts at the system's edge, its crystalline antenna arrays generating visible distortion halos in vacuum. The station rotates slowly, tracking signals from civilizations light-years distant.

Alien megastructure orbits a neutron star, its purpose unknowable and builders extinct. Volcanic rock fused to impossible geometries, the structure survived radiation that melted nearby asteroids to slag.

Collapsed alien ringworld fragments form a debris field around a dead star, organic-looking architecture overgrown with crystalline formations. Exploration ships navigate carefully through ruins larger than moons.

Warp gate floats at the Lagrange point, its aperture wide enough to swallow battleships whole. Energy crackles through the ring's interior, distorting starlight into impossible colors as ships transit.

Ancient jump ring orbits a black hole's event horizon, gravitational lensing creating mirror images around its corroded frame. The gate's alien symbols still glow faintly, beckoning to unknown destinations.

O'Neill cylinder rotates in lunar orbit, interior landscape visible through transparent endcaps showing cities, farmland, and weather. Clouds drift across the cylinder's inner surface, casting shadows upward.

Damaged habitat cylinder vents atmosphere through hull breaches, its interior ecosystem freezing in vacuum's embrace. Through cracked view-ports, frozen lakes and forests glitter under emergency lighting.

Stellar collector extends vast heat-shielded petals into a blue giant's corona, harvesting plasma through magnetic containment. The structure glows white-hot, radiating excess energy across the electromagnetic spectrum.
Automated harvester siphons material from a red dwarf, its living-metal tendrils drinking stellar matter like roots. Tanker ships dock at collection nodes, dwarfed by the infrastructure feeding them.

Observatory array orbits beyond the heliopause, lens assemblies wider than Earth pointed at distant galaxies. The telescope's ceramic-plated mirror surface reflects cosmic background radiation in false-color beauty.
Ancient alien telescope drifts inert, its planet-scale lens cracked and dark. Whatever civilization built this eye to pierce the cosmos left only this monument to their celestial curiosity.

Prison fortress hangs isolated in interstellar void, layered defense grids visible as geometric energy patterns. No planets, no sun—just the facility and its endless watch over whatever horrors dwell within.

Maximum security station occupies a hollowed asteroid, blast doors larger than skyscrapers sealing each containment level. Guard ships patrol ceaselessly, their running lights tracing patterns around the prison's darkness.

Dyson bubble envelops a neutron star, mesh of carbon-composite panels harvesting incredible energies. Magnetic storms visible across the structure's surface discharge lightning bolts between collection nodes spanning continents.

Ringworld wraps a black hole's accretion disk, impossible materials withstanding gravitational stress. Time dilation creates visible aurora along the ring's inner edge where physics strains against engineering.

Orbital elevator's anchor station dominates the planet's equator, megacity sprawling outward from the tether's base. Storm systems form around the cable, weather patterns permanently altered by its presence.

Massive station complex extends across an entire planetary orbit, interconnected modules forming a necklace around the world. Each habitat houses millions, their collective lights visible from the planet's nightside.

Generation ship enters target system after millennial voyage, external hull weathered by interstellar radiation. Deceleration burns illuminate the vessel's massive drive arrays, brighter than the distant sun ahead.

Orbital construction facility assembles habitats from asteroid material, dozens of stations in various completion stages. The drydock itself spans hundreds of kilometers, its framework visible against the nebula beyond.

Defense grid surrounds a fortress world, weapons platforms linked by fire-control networks spanning millions of kilometers. Battle debris clouds the spaces between stations, testament to repelled invasions.

Rogue mining platform tears apart a water-ice comet, extraction beams visible as coherent light against the expanding vapor cloud. The rig's processing core glows red-hot, sublimating ice to access metallic core.

Communications nexus floats at the galaxy's center, relay dishes receiving and transmitting messages across vast distances. Maintenance scaffolding crawls the antenna surfaces, workers visible as microscopic dots.

Derelict alien station tumbles through space, its builders gone but automated systems still maintaining mysterious functions. Bioluminescent patterns pulse across the structure's organic-looking hull in hypnotic waves.
Warp gate network spans a star cluster, jump rings positioned at gravitational nexus points. Energy discharges arc between gates when synchronized, creating temporary bridges across the cosmic void.

O'Neill cylinder farm orbits a gas giant, dozens of habitat tubes rotating in synchronized formation. Agricultural sections glow green through transparent panels, feeding populations across the entire system.

Stellar forge draws material from a dying star's outer layers, feeding exotic matter into massive fabrication chambers. Whatever emerges from those construction bays requires stellar energies to manufacture.

Observatory platform peers into a dimensional rift, instrument arrays analyzing impossible physics. The telescope's housing is heavily shielded, protecting operators from whatever radiates from that cosmic wound.

Quarantine facility orbits a plague world, its isolation protocols enforced by automated weapon systems. Nothing enters or leaves without permission; the station's inhabitants know only sterile halls and eternal vigilance.

Dyson swarm under construction surrounds a young star, assembly drones weaving new panels into the growing array. Completed sections already generate visible power transmission beams to orbital receivers.

Ancient ring fragments drift in decaying orbits, their interior surfaces exposed to vacuum and stellar wind. Xenoarchaeologists explore carefully, aware entire cities exist as frozen ruins on those inner shores.
Space tether connects twin planets in tidal lock, cargo pods traversing the cable between worlds. The structure's midpoint station experiences true zero-gravity, serving as system-wide trade hub.

Station megalopolis extends across a moon's orbit, modular sections added over centuries creating chaotic architectural sprawl. Ship traffic flows through designated corridors, organized chaos against the structure's vastness.

World-ship approaches red dwarf after centuries of flight, deceleration causing visible atmospheric disturbance in its bow habitat sections. The vessel's wake glows with expelled reaction mass, a comet of human ambition.

Partial Dyson shell cups a blue supergiant's equator, triangular panels flexing under radiation pressure. Repair ships glide between segments, their heat signatures barely visible against million-degree stellar winds.

Shattered Dyson framework orbits a pulsar, living-metal girders pulsing in rhythm with electromagnetic bursts. The structure's broken symmetry creates lighthouse-like shadows sweeping across surrounding debris fields.

Ringworld arcs around a binary star system, its inner surface reflecting dual sunrises across continental panels. Atmospheric bands swirl visibly where weather systems meet at the structure's rotational boundaries.

Corroded orbital ring fragments a gas giant's equator, gaps revealing interior trusses thicker than mountain ranges. Scavenger fleets harvest metal between stabilizer nodes, ant-like against the architecture's decay.

Orbital tether rises from ocean platform, its graphene cable nearly invisible except where clouds refract around it. Ascending cargo containers catch sunlight like ascending stars against the darkening altitude gradient.

Broken space elevator dangles from orbital station, severed cable coiling across planetary surface in kilometers-long spirals. Storms form around the fallen sections, their mass permanently affecting regional climate patterns.

Wheel-station rotates above ringed planet, spoke-connections thick as city districts transferring goods between spinning habitats. Docking traffic spirals inward following navigational beacons, timed precisely to rotational cycles.

Abandoned station complex drifts through comet halo, ceramic hull plates cratered by millennia of micrometeorite impacts. Frozen atmosphere trails from breaches like ghostly banners, crystals glittering in starlight.

Generation ark crosses stellar void, mountain ranges visible through transparent dome covering one hemisphere. Lightning flickers between artificial peaks, storm systems cycling through the enclosed biosphere.

Colony ship decelerates toward new star, its rotating habitat drums creating visible gyroscopic wobble in approach trajectory. Ice shields sublimate during braking burns, shrouding the vessel in temporary comet-tail.

Shipyard constructs capital ships across parallel berths, each vessel's keel longer than orbital stations. Material conveyors feed construction zones from asteroid processors, streams of refined metal flowing like arterial blood.

Derelict drydock orbits neutron star, its carbon-composite framework twisted by tidal forces but somehow intact. Half-finished warship hulls remain clamped in berths, their crews extinct before launch ceremonies.

Weapons platform dominates dwarf planet's orbit, targeting arrays tracking every object within light-seconds. Battle scars pit the fortress's crystalline armor, fused craters marking where boarding actions failed.

Ancient defense grid surrounds sterile world, automated stations maintaining geometric formation despite controller civilization's extinction. Periodically they fire on nothing, targeting ghosts in corrupted sensor memory.

Mining rig consumes asteroid belt, processing maws grinding planetesimals to powder and extracting rare elements. The industrial complex leaves geometric void in the belt, absence visible as dark gap in debris field.

Ore extraction platform straddles volcanic moon, drilling through crust to tap mantle's mineral wealth. Magma fountains erupt around boring sites, cooling into twisted spires that dwarf the harvesting machinery.

Communication nexus crowns airless world, dish farms covering entire hemisphere pointed at galactic core. Coolant systems create artificial ice fields around equipment, their crystalline structure visible from orbit.

Relay station orbits rogue planet in interstellar darkness, its antenna arrays the only light for light-years. Signal traffic creates visible distortion patterns as transmissions bend spacetime through sheer data density.

Alien monument surrounds brown dwarf, architectural logic incomprehensible to human observers but clearly purposeful. The structure's shadow patterns suggest calendar functions, measuring cosmic cycles across deep time.

Overgrown megastructure drifts at system's edge, crystalline growths replacing damaged sections in bizarre organic mimicry. Whatever built this is gone, but the structure somehow maintains itself through unknown processes.

Jump gate hovers near planetary nebula, aperture framing newborn stars in its massive ring. Transit flashes illuminate the gate's inner surfaces, energy discharges creating temporary constellations before fading.

Corroded warp ring orbits white dwarf, its activation sequence still broadcasting on ancient frequencies. Ships avoid the structure despite operational status, records warning of destinations none returned from.

O'Neill cylinder cluster orbits ice giant, dozens of habitats linked by transit tubes visible as fine threads. Agricultural cylinders glow green with chlorophyll, feeding industrial sections where city lights burn constant.

Battle-damaged habitat drum tumbles unpowered, its rotation stopped by warfare that shattered structural governors. Through breach-holes, frozen bodies and buildings create grim diorama of life interrupted.

Telescope array floats in Oort cloud, mirror assemblies wider than continents focusing on distant quasars. The observatory's ceramic surfaces reflect cosmic background radiation, nearly invisible against space's darkness.

Quarantine station hangs in deep space, automated systems maintaining eternal watch over frozen disease samples. Guard ships patrol in century-long shifts, their crews forbidden planetfall for contamination risk.

Gate network node connects forty-seven warp rings in hub formation, their apertures facing outward like flower petals. Simultaneous transits create cascading energy effects, temporary Einstein-Rosen bridges manifesting as lightning.

Containment facility occupies hollow comet nucleus, isolation protocols requiring light-minutes separation from inhabited zones. Nothing living enters or exits, automated systems maintaining sterile vigil over samples best forgotten.

Swarm panels self-replicate around young star, exponential growth visible as expanding shell consuming construction material asteroids. The automated network will eventually enclose the star completely without biological oversight.

Ring fragments maintain partial orbit despite catastrophic structural failure, artificial gravity still functioning in intact sections. Expeditions explore carefully, aware the ruins could complete their collapse any century.

Metropolitan station sprawls across planetary orbit, construction styles spanning centuries creating architectural archaeology in three dimensions. Some sectors predate FTL technology, built when this was humanity's furthest reach.

Colony ark approaches brown dwarf, its passengers' descendants preparing to settle captured rogue planets in permanent darkness. Habitat lighting creates false day-night cycles visible as pulsing glow through transparent hull sections.

Dyson swarm fragments orbit orange dwarf in stable configuration, billions of solar panels forming incomplete lattice around the star. Freighters navigate the gaps, their heat signatures barely distinguishable from the stellar background radiation.

Crystalline Dyson framework surrounds variable star, energy collectors flexing to compensate for luminosity fluctuations. The structure's transparent sections refract starlight into rainbow arcs visible across neighboring systems.

Ringworld encircles white star at habitable distance, agricultural bands alternating with urban zones across interior surface. Three captured asteroids serve as anchoring counterweights, their orbital corrections creating visible thruster plumes.

Orbital elevator penetrates stratocumulus deck, bio-metal cable regenerating damage from lightning strikes in real-time. Climber pods ascend through storm layers, their running lights creating pearl-string effect through cloud gaps.

Spoke-wheel station dominates lunar orbit, each residential arm longer than terrestrial megacities rotating for artificial gravity. Thermal radiators glow cherry-red along the spokes, dissipating heat from millions of inhabitants.

Frozen station tumbles through Kuiper belt, its carbon-composite hull coated in ammonia frost layers meters thick. Emergency beacons still pulse weakly, lights flickering in windows where backup power maintains spectral existence.

Generation vessel crosses dark nebula, forests visible through dome sections glowing with bioluminescent understory. Exterior hull carries impact craters deep enough to swallow city blocks, century scars from micrometeorite bombardment.

Sleeper ship rotates for stability during coast phase, its living sections dim while most passengers remain in cryogenic suspension. Navigation lights trace geometric patterns across the hull, automated systems maintaining lonely watch.

Shipyard constructs carriers in tiered berths, skeletal frameworks extending across construction zone like metallic ribcages. Welding drones number in thousands, their collective light creating artificial constellation against planetary shadow.

Derelict construction facility orbits backward through stellar wind, its assembly berths holding corroded ship hulls abandoned mid-construction. Solar radiation has welded some frames to docking clamps, rust and metal fused permanently.

Gun platform orbits barren world, weapon bore diameters wide enough to swallow corvettes whole. Targeting arrays swivel constantly, tracking debris field objects with paranoid precision suggesting combat-corrupted threat assessment protocols.

Decaying defense perimeter surrounds garden world, stations maintaining formation despite obvious power failures. Their weapons track randomly, firing solutions cycling through targets that existed when their creators still lived.

Extraction rig straddles rocky moon, boring apparatus penetrating to core and drawing molten metals through thermal pipelines. Heat distortion surrounds processing towers, atmosphere shimmering above equipment that glows furnace-bright.

Overgrown ringworld fragments orbit cold white dwarf, silicon-based life coating interior surfaces in crystalline forests. The structures glow faintly with stored thermal energy, bio-luminescence creating false stars across ruins.

Dyson shell section cups red giant's pole, triangular panels angled to catch stellar wind's magnetic component. Construction continues autonomously, assembly drones adding panels to structure already spanning millions of square kilometers.

Counter-rotating habitat drums nearly touch at endcaps, sharing atmosphere and resources through connection at rotation axis. Transfer tubes carry traffic through zero-gravity nexus, bustling with cross-cylinder commerce.

Stellar manufactory orbits within chromosphere, construction bays using star's heat for exotic material synthesis. Finished products emerge glowing, atomic structures impossible to replicate at lower temperatures.

Observatory platform pierces dimensional membrane, instruments probing parallel realities through carefully maintained breach. Exotic radiation containment fails occasionally, strange light reflecting off structure's polished surfaces in impossible colors.

Containment station occupies comet nucleus, isolation requiring light-minutes separation from inhabited zones. Nothing living enters or exits, automated systems maintaining sterile vigil over samples best forgotten by sane civilizations.
